hard.next_step: Alix Luc granted Zycus shared-space access (31 Mar 2026); prior: 2-hour deep-dive demo delivered 21 Jan 2026 covering accounting module, SAP integration and a DORA module, 'well received'.
hard.ais_why: Product scope is purely traditional S2P (eInvoicing, eProcurement, iContract, iSave, iSource, iSupplier, iCompliance) with zero AI modules; across 3 Avoma meetings the customer repeatedly pivoted Zycus's AI/Merlin pitches back to cost, DORA compliance and SAP integration.

north_star_verdict.headline: Budget-approved full S2P replacement remains on a formal RFP shortlist that Vincent Buelens re-confirmed in writing on 23 Jun, but the deal has produced only administrative touches since the 21 Jan demo (portal access 31 Mar, RFP-inclusion email 23 Jun), is single-threaded through the procurement manager, and our AI-led pitch keeps missing a buyer focused on DORA, SAP S/4HANA and cost.
north_star_verdict.math: Close date 2027-03-30 (~267 days out). Created / qualified 2025-12-08 (~210 days old). Stage Formal Evaluation since ~Dec 2025 = ~7 months in stage, normal for a 12-15 month enterprise S2P cycle. Last buyer activity 2026-06-23 (13 days ago: Vincent Buelens email confirming Zycus's inclusion in the P&V Source2Pay RFP). Deep-dive use-case demo 2026-01-21 (~5.5 months ago). No forward slip on the close date since the 2007->2027 typo correction on 27 Apr. Pace is deliberate but thin: no new meeting, no commercial submission, no shortlist down-select in 5.5 months, so momentum is Slowing rather than On Track, while the recent RFP-inclusion confirmation keeps it well clear of Off Track.
evidence: Last verified activity 2026-06-23 (13 days ago): Vincent Buelens emailed 'Yes confirmed, thanks for including Zycus' on the P&V RFP Procurement/Source2Pay thread, cc Andres Orduz (source: Task 2026-06-23) - LIVE but rep-adjacent engagement.
Formal RFP is the live gate: Zycus is confirmed included but the RFP has not yet been formally issued/scored and no shortlist down-select has occurred (source: Task 2026-06-23).

2-hour use-case demo 2026-01-21 covered supplier risk/DORA compliance, sourcing, iContract, eProcurement and eInvoicing/PEPPOL to a ~15-person buyer committee; wrap-up 'it was quite a good session', 'very helpful' (source: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21).
Only administrative movement since the demo: Alix Luc granted Zycus shared-space access 2026-03-31 (source: Task 2026-03-31 / Next_Step).

Budget already approved and project is the new procurement manager's top priority: Vincent Buelens 'we already have the budget... my top priority for next year' (source: Avoma discovery call 2025-12-08, carried forward).

AI narrative rejected: Vincent 'maybe for now we can just follow the classic way' of working, AIS_Status Negative, product scope carries zero AI modules (source: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21 + AIS field).

Single-threaded through Vincent Buelens; finance director Luk De Troch, who supervises the project and owns the budget, has not been directly engaged by Zycus (source: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21).

Trajectory holding STEADY vs prior 'Slowing' sweep, marginally strengthening on the 23 Jun RFP-inclusion confirmation; the single biggest blocker to close is the slow, single-threaded formal RFP with no commercial motion yet.

metrics.narrative: Vincent Buelens (Procurement Manager) articulated the problem on 2025-12-08: P&V Group runs no procurement tool at all, managing all contracts and spend manually ('it's quite a mess'), across an estimated EUR 100-150M annual indirect spend and ~1,000 suppliers (70% IT, 20% consultancy). The most concrete, quantifiable pain surfaced on 2026-01-21: the DORA 'register of information' (ITS register) for the National Bank of Belgium, which Vincent said 'we need to report each year... it's really an odd thing that we need to do manually now, and that takes us a lot of time'. Cost leakage is acknowledged ('I already know that I'm paying way too much for a lot of contracts', 08 Dec) but no target savings %, cycle-time or headcount ROI figure has been stated. The value case is qualitative and regulatory, not yet a quantified savings model, so quantifying it against DORA reporting hours and contract cost leakage is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21 (Vincent Buelens, DORA ITS register)

economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is inferable from the calls even though no OpportunityContactRole role is stamped: on 2026-01-21 Vincent introduced 'Luke... who is our finance director, we'll supervise all the project of the new procurement tool', matching Salesforce contact Luk De Troch, Director of Finance and Operations. Budget authority is already exercised: Vincent confirmed on 2025-12-08 the project is approved and funded ('we already have the budget'). A finance stakeholder (most likely Luk De Troch) closed the demo asking 'Caroline, when can you deliver us some more figures with euros' and flagged 'expect some kind of a reduction anyway' (21 Jan), showing the commercial decision sits with finance. The gap is not identification but access: Zycus has no direct relationship with Luk De Troch and reaches the budget owner only through Vincent. Assign Luk De Troch the Economic Buyer role and open a direct finance conversation before the RFP scores.
sources: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21 (Vincent Buelens introducing Luk De Troch; finance director on pricing)
OpportunityContactRole: De Troch Luk, Director of Finance and Operations

decision_criteria.narrative: The 2026-01-21 demo made the evaluation criteria explicit and buyer-stated. Vincent set the agenda: 'the focus for us is on 3 different topics: first DORA, then integration to SAP S/4HANA, and the payments/billing/e-invoicing'. DORA compliance is criterion one (supplier risk assessments, subcontractor chains for critical-and-important-function suppliers, and automatic extraction of the ITS register for the National Bank of Belgium). SAP integration is criterion two, with Andres Orduz probing R/3 (ECC) vs S/4HANA adapter compatibility, data-flow architecture and source-of-truth ownership. eInvoicing/PEPPOL compliance is criterion three (certified access point, Belgian structured e-invoices, VAT auto-configuration). Cross-cutting criteria: ease of use / minimal customization, configurable reporting with CSV/Excel/PDF export, multi-language (Flemish/French), and cost-effectiveness ('I don't need a Rolls Royce'). These are confirmed from named speakers on the transcript, not inferred.
sources: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21 (Vincent Buelens: DORA/SAP/e-invoicing focus; Andres Orduz: SAP integration and reporting)

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal RFP that Vincent authored after his own AIRFI research, with a stated shortlist of max 3-4 vendors from ~5 initial candidates (08 Dec), and Zycus's inclusion re-confirmed 2026-06-23. The 2026-01-21 call reveals the buying committee: Vincent Buelens (procurement, process owner), Luk De Troch (finance director, oversight/budget), Andres Orduz (TCNR / technical-architecture evaluator driving integration and reporting questions), Mickel Michielsen (Chief Product Owner Finance/Risk/Procurement), and Nathalie Maes (accounting). Budget approval already cleared internally, so the remaining process is RFP issuance, vendor response, scoring and down-select. The unknowns are the RFP's formal timeline, evaluation weighting and who signs off the selection, all of which should be pulled from Vincent while the thread is warm.

paper_process.narrative: This deal is at Formal Evaluation, well short of contracting, so paper mechanics are largely undefined. Commercial model is known from 2026-01-21: user-based licensing weighted on power users (procurement/sourcing team) with negligible cost for business users, invoices priced per matched-invoice volume, plus a one-time implementation cost; standard implementation quoted at 6-8 months including one month hypercare. No MSA, Order Form or SOW has been drafted, no legal or InfoSec review scheduled, and there is no incumbent contract expiration to act as a forcing function (P&V has no procurement tool today). As a Belgian BFSI (insurance) buyer under DORA, expect a heavy downstream paper load: DORA contractual clauses, DPA/GDPR, InfoSec/security questionnaire and subcontractor disclosure. Visibility here is created by pulling the RFP's contracting and compliance requirements from Vincent now.
sources: Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21 (pricing model, 6-8 month implementation)

identify_pain.narrative: Confirmed pain, owner Vincent Buelens: P&V manages all procurement, spend and ~1,000 supplier contracts manually with no tool ('quite a mess', 08 Dec), producing no spend visibility and heavy manual effort. The sharpest, regulator-driven pain is DORA: manually assembling the ITS register of ICT contracts (values, dates, notice periods, subcontractor chains, LEI/VAT numbers) for the National Bank of Belgium each year, which Vincent called an 'odd thing we do manually that takes a lot of time' (21 Jan). Andres Orduz named a second operational pain: consultant service-entry-sheets/timesheets validated through 'different tools, different Excels' and manual copy-paste into SAP, which he flagged as high-value to automate but internally 'politically sensitive'. Inferred pain (from stack + SAP S/4HANA migration): P&V must land the tool in step with the S/4HANA programme, adding timing pressure.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-12-08 (Vincent Buelens, manual mess)
Avoma demo transcript 2026-01-21 (Vincent: DORA ITS register; Andres Orduz: timesheet/service-entry-sheet pain)

champion.narrative: Vincent Buelens (Procurement Manager) is a developing champion. What has developed: he sourced Zycus himself, placed and kept it on the RFP shortlist, gave positive demo feedback ('well received', 21 Jan), re-confirmed inclusion on 23 Jun, provisioned shared-space access for his team, and previously downgraded Coupa and SAP Ariba as too expensive. What is still missing to make him strong: no written endorsement, no introduction of Zycus to the finance/budget owner Luk De Troch, no multi-threading into legal or the business, and he told us on 08 Dec he is 'quite alone to handle all the RFP process'. That solo posture is both a simplification (single influence point) and a risk (no internal selling, thin cover if a rival multi-threads). He is engaged but not yet advocating; building him and widening the thread is the priority.

competition.narrative: P&V is running a formal RFP with a shortlist of max 3-4 vendors from ~5 initial candidates. Named rivals (from the 08 Dec discovery call, carried forward - no new competitor evidence surfaced this sweep): Ivalua, Coupa, SAP Ariba and GEP. The buyer has no incumbent tool, so the alternatives are pure new-vendor competition plus status-quo manual process. Recency-weighted, Ivalua is the strongest current peer threat (mature S2P platform with financial-services traction, but exposed on single-tenant change-order cost), Coupa and SAP Ariba were both explicitly downgraded by Vincent on price ('Ariba is a dinosaur... we won't pay for that'), and GEP is the configure-to-order alternative. Zycus's wedge is affordability plus a composable multi-tenant model, out-of-box S/4HANA connector, demoed DORA compliance, and PEPPOL/certified-access-point coverage; the gate is RFP scoring, so re-anchoring on DORA + SAP + cost (not AI) is how we win.
sources: Avoma discovery call 2025-12-08 (Vincent Buelens + Caroline Lacocque, competitor shortlist) - carried forward

deal_movement.summary: Live but slow. Since the 21 Jan deep-dive demo the only movement is the 31 Mar shared-space access grant and the 23 Jun email in which Vincent Buelens re-confirmed Zycus's inclusion in the formal Source2Pay RFP. No new meeting, no commercial proposal, no shortlist down-select in 5.5 months; the close date was corrected from a 2007 typo to 2027-03-30 on 27 Apr (data hygiene, not a slip).

items.change: Vincent Buelens re-confirmed Zycus's inclusion in the P&V Source2Pay RFP by email ('Yes confirmed, thanks for including Zycus')
Close date corrected from erroneous 2007-03-30 to 2027-03-30 (data fix, no real slip)
Alix Luc (Enterprise Architect) granted Zycus shared-space access for technical review
2-hour use-case demo delivered to ~15-person committee: DORA/supplier risk, sourcing, iContract, eProcurement, eInvoicing/PEPPOL, reporting

competitive_position.summary: P&V is running a formal RFP with a shortlist of max 3-4 suppliers from ~5 candidates (Zycus confirmed in on 23 Jun). No new competitor evidence surfaced this sweep, so threat levels are carried forward from the 08 Dec discovery call: the single strongest CURRENT threat is IVALUA, the live enterprise S2P peer with financial-services traction competing on completeness but exposed on single-tenant change-order cost. Coupa is a lower current threat (Vincent 'won't pay for such a solution', support/PE-ownership doubts reinforced by ex-Coupa Caroline). SAP Ariba is effectively out ('a dinosaur', priced out). GEP is the configure-to-order alternative with longer time-to-value. The real secondary threat is inertia only insofar as scope creep in the RFP; budget is approved and this is a must-do, so status-quo is a low risk. Zycus wins on affordability + composable multi-tenant + out-of-box S/4HANA + demoed DORA + PEPPOL/certified access point.

competitors.how_we_win: Already priced out and rejected by the buyer as too complex/expensive; no active threat, hold position on affordability + fit.

best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers for a slow, single-threaded, price-sensitive DORA-driven RFP where our AI story is landing badly.
flags: Retire the AI/Merlin lead: the buyer pivoted back to 'the classic way' and AIS is Negative, so anchor the RFP response on DORA compliance, SAP S/4HANA fit and cost, not agentic AI.
Multi-thread past Vincent: get in front of finance director Luk De Troch (economic buyer) and Mickel Michielsen (Finance/Risk) before the RFP is scored, since Vincent is working the RFP alone.
Own the DORA ITS-register extraction use case: it is the buyer's most-emphasized unresolved regulatory pain and a concrete differentiator vs generic S2P rivals.

items.title: Director of Finance and Operations
items.sentiment: Neutral-positive: supervises the project and pressed for euro figures ('expect some kind of a reduction anyway')
items.risk: Mapped and budget approved, but Zycus has no direct relationship; reached only through Vincent

champion_strength.summary: Vincent Buelens is a developing champion: he sourced Zycus, keeps it on the RFP shortlist (re-confirmed 23 Jun), gave positive demo feedback and provisioned team access, and previously excluded Coupa/Ariba on price. What is still missing to make him strong: no written endorsement, no introduction to budget owner Luk De Troch, no multi-threading to legal/business, and he is self-described as working the RFP alone. Active but not yet advocating; at risk if a rival multi-threads while we stay single-threaded.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus is OVER-positioning AI, not under-positioning it. Nishita and Caroline repeatedly led with Merlin intake and Gen-AI on 21 Jan, but Vincent steered back to 'the classic way of working' and the customer never engaged the AI narrative in any of the three meetings. The winning move is to de-emphasize agentic AI and lead with DORA, SAP S/4HANA and cost.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS_Score 2.0 / Negative, and the calls corroborate rather than under-rate it: across three meetings the buyer actively deflected the AI/Merlin pitch back to cost, DORA compliance and SAP integration ('maybe for now we can just follow the classic way', 21 Jan), and the product scope contains zero AI modules. This is a traditional, regulation-driven S2P evaluation, not an AI-led one.

items.detail: Economic buyer Luk De Troch (finance director, budget owner) is mapped but not directly engaged by Zycus; the deal runs entirely through Vincent.
Price-sensitive buyer ('not a Rolls Royce', 'expect a reduction') with no commercial proposal yet delivered in a live 3-4 vendor RFP.
Legacy SAP ECC (R/3) integration was left uncommitted by Zycus during the S/4HANA migration window; unresolved technical risk for Andres Orduz.
Single-threaded through Vincent, who is working the RFP alone; and consultant timesheet/service-entry-sheet automation is internally 'politically sensitive'.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live but thin: recent touch (23 Jun RFP confirmation) is a rep-adjacent email, not a fresh meeting or pull; no call in 5.5 months and no commercial motion. Cadence is slow but not cold.
items: Positive: budget approved, top priority, Zycus re-confirmed on the RFP shortlist 23 Jun.
Watch: no new meeting since 21 Jan; only administrative/email touches since.
Watch: AI pitch actively rejected; must re-anchor on DORA/SAP/cost.

scope_change.from: Full Source-to-Pay suite (iSource, iContract, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iCompliance)
scope_change.detail: Scope and amount ($250k) are unchanged versus the prior record; product set remains the full traditional S2P suite with zero AI modules. No narrowing or expansion detected this sweep.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: P&V Group's new procurement manager has Zycus on a budget-approved formal RFP shortlist for a DORA-driven S2P replacement, but the deal is slow and single-threaded through Vincent, price-sensitive, and our AI-led pitch keeps missing a buyer who wants DORA, SAP S/4HANA and cost, not Merlin.

win_position.text: We sit on a budget-approved formal RFP shortlist that Vincent Buelens re-confirmed in writing on 23 Jun after a well-received 2-hour DORA/SAP demo (21 Jan), but we are mid-pack in a 3-4 vendor field with no commercial submitted.
Our AI-agent narrative is actively rejected: Vincent pivoted back to 'the classic way of working' on 21 Jan and AIS is Negative, so leaning on Merlin loses the room.
We are single-threaded through Vincent and have no direct relationship with finance director Luk De Troch, who supervises the project and owns the already-approved budget (21 Jan).

deal_momentum.text: Only administrative touches since the 21 Jan demo, a portal-access grant on 31 Mar and an RFP-inclusion email on 23 Jun, with no new meeting, commercial or shortlist down-select in ~5.5 months.
The 23 Jun RFP-inclusion confirmation shows the formal evaluation is now live and Zycus is still in it.

customer_commitment.text: Budget is approved and the tool is the new procurement manager's stated top priority (Vincent, 08 Dec), and he keeps Zycus in the RFP (23 Jun).
Commitment is capped by price sensitivity ('not a Rolls Royce', 'expect some kind of a reduction') and a competitive RFP still open to 3-4 vendors.

deal_risk.text: SAP S/4HANA versus legacy ECC (R/3) adapter fit is unconfirmed: Nishita could not commit on ECC integration on 21 Jan.
The consultant timesheet / service-entry-sheet process is internally 'politically sensitive' (Andres Orduz, 21 Jan), a change-management landmine on scope.

deal_momentum.evidence: No meeting or commercial in 5.5 months; only a portal grant (31 Mar) and an RFP-inclusion email (23 Jun).

customer_preference.evidence: On the shortlist and re-confirmed, but running a competitive RFP with no decisive Zycus-specific lean this period.

deal_risk.evidence: AI rejection, single-thread, unconfirmed ECC integration, price sensitivity.

customer_preference.evidence: Zycus kept on the RFP shortlist and re-confirmed 23 Jun ('thanks for including Zycus'), positive 21 Jan demo, but no decisive Zycus-specific preference over Ivalua/GEP this period and clear price sensitivity.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative and regulatory pain only (manual procurement 'a mess', DORA ITS-register reporting burden, 'paying way too much'); no quantified savings %, cycle-time or headcount ROI stated.
